title: Nabarangpur Schools and Colleges Closed from April 20 Amid Heatwave - Pragativadi

full_text:
Nabarangpur district administration announced a holiday for all educational institutions starting April 20 as temperatures touched 44°C.
District Collector issued an official order directing Anganwadi Centres, schools, colleges, and technical institutions to remain closed until further instructions. The decision came after the district recorded extreme heatwave conditions that posed risks to students and staff.
Authorities confirmed that examinations already scheduled during this period will continue. Institutions may shift exam timings to morning hours to avoid peak heat. The Collector instructed heads of institutions to ensure adequate drinking water facilities and provide heatstroke prevention arrangements for students appearing in exams.
The administration emphasised strict compliance with the directive. Schools and colleges must maintain shaded areas and hydration points. The move aims to safeguard children and staff from heat-related illnesses.
Western Odisha districts, including Nabarangpur, continue to experience soaring temperatures above 40°C. Similar closures have been announced in other districts to protect students from extreme weather.

title: Severe Heatwave Forces Nabarangapur Admin To Shut Educational Institutions From Apr 20 - Ommcom News

full_text:
Bhubaneswar: In response to the intensifying heatwave conditions sweeping across Odisha, the Nabarangapur District Collector has directed the closure of all educational institutions in the district starting from April 20 until further orders.
According to the official order issued by the district administration, all Anganwadi Centres (AWCs), government and private schools, colleges, and technical institutions will remain shut to safeguard the health and well-being of students, teachers, and staff from the extreme summer heat.
The decision comes as western and southern districts of Odisha, including Nabarangapur, continue to experience scorching daytime temperatures, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) issuing warnings for heatwave conditions in several parts of the state. Temperatures in many interior districts have already crossed 40°C, and a further rise is expected in the coming days. However, examinations already scheduled during this period will continue as planned.
The district administration has advised institutions to reschedule exams to early morning hours wherever possible to avoid the peak heat of the day. Heads of all educational institutions have been strictly directed to ensure the availability of adequate drinking water facilities and implement necessary heat-wave safety measures.
